arrivalSteps.title: Visa Verification / Visa on Arrival
arrivalSteps.description: Citizens of 80+ countries receive a free visa on arrival valid for 30–90 days (varies by nationality). Check the ICP portal (icp.gov.ae) for your country's eligibility. Other nationalities must hold a pre-arranged UAE visa. Passport must have at least 6 months validity.

arrivalSteps.title: Smart Gate / Passport Control
arrivalSteps.description: UAE citizens and registered residents use Smart Gates with Emirates ID. Tourists queue at manual immigration counters or e-Gates if enabled for their passport. Present your passport and boarding pass; an entry stamp is issued upon clearance.

arrivalSteps.title: Biometric Enrollment
arrivalSteps.description: All first-time visitors provide iris scans and fingerprints at the immigration counter. These are stored against your passport for future visits and linked to your visa record.

arrivalSteps.title: Baggage Claim
arrivalSteps.description: Collect your luggage from the designated carousel. UAE airports use a monitored baggage exit; retain your claim stubs as they may be checked.

arrivalSteps.title: Customs Declaration
arrivalSteps.description: Duty-free allowance: 4L of alcohol (or 2 cases of beer) for non-Muslims aged 18+, 400 cigarettes, gifts up to AED 3,000. Declare cash over AED 100,000. Prohibited: narcotics, pork products (at some ports), sexually explicit material, items from sanctioned countries. Penalties are severe.

arrivalSteps.title: Exit to Arrivals Hall
arrivalSteps.description: After customs clearance, proceed to the arrivals hall. Taxis, pre-booked rides (Careem, Uber), and the Dubai Metro are available from Dubai International (DXB). Keep your passport accessible as spot checks occur near the exit.

fallbackAlerts.message: Carrying even trace amounts of illegal drugs — including cannabis — results in mandatory imprisonment under UAE law.
DVDs, books, or media with sexual content are illegal to import. They will be confiscated and may result in a fine.

scenarios.description: Dress code, prayer times, and conduct inside mosques

arrivalSteps.title: Dress Code is Mandatory
arrivalSteps.description: Women: full-length abaya covering arms and legs, head scarf required. Men: long trousers, covered shoulders.

arrivalSteps.title: Remove Shoes Before Entering
arrivalSteps.description: Leave all footwear at the entrance. Carry them with you or leave at the shoe rack.

arrivalSteps.title: Respect Prayer Times
arrivalSteps.description: If prayer begins, stand quietly to the side. Do not walk in front of or disturb people praying.

fallbackAlerts.message: Both men and women must be modestly dressed to enter a mosque. Abayas are provided at the Grand Mosque entrance.
Photography is usually allowed in common areas of grand mosques, but never during prayer or inside the prayer hall without permission.

phrases.english: Is there a dress code here?
Please show me where to enter.
phrases.nativeScript: أرشدني إلى مدخل الزوار من فضلك.
phrases.romanized: Arshidni ila madkhal al-zuwwar min fadlak.

scenarios.description: Taxi etiquette, app rides, and pricing

arrivalSteps.title: Use Metered or App Taxis
arrivalSteps.description: All licensed RTA taxis in Dubai are metered. Careem and Uber are widely available and reliable.

arrivalSteps.title: Seat Belt is Mandatory
arrivalSteps.description: Seat belts for all passengers are legally required and strictly enforced.

fallbackAlerts.message: Public displays of affection in taxis and in public are illegal under UAE law.
Dress modestly when using taxis — revealing clothing can result in complaints or legal issues.

phrases.english: Please take me to the Dubai Mall.
phrases.nativeScript: خذني إلى دبي مول من فضلك.
phrases.romanized: Khudni ila Dubai Mall min fadlak.

scenarios.description: Beach rules, dress code, and prohibited behaviours

arrivalSteps.title: Swimwear Only in the Water
arrivalSteps.description: Swimwear is acceptable at designated public beaches only. Cover up when walking away from the beach.

arrivalSteps.title: No Drinking Alcohol
arrivalSteps.description: Drinking alcohol on public beaches is illegal and can result in arrest.

fallbackAlerts.message: Consuming alcohol on a public beach is illegal in the UAE. Alcohol is only permitted in licensed venues.
Kissing and embracing in public — including on the beach — is illegal and can result in arrest.

scenarios.description: UAE passport control and legal requirements

arrivalSteps.title: Israel Passport Note
arrivalSteps.description: The UAE now accepts Israeli passports after the Abraham Accords normalisation.

arrivalSteps.title: Visa on Arrival for Many Nationals
arrivalSteps.description: Many nationalities receive a free 30-day visa on arrival. Check your eligibility before travel.

fallbackAlerts.message: Individuals with outstanding debt judgements in the UAE may be detained at immigration. Check your status before travelling.
